title: Tangy Fermented Korean Vegetable Bowl
description: A fermented and springy Korean dish featuring wholegrain mustard and served in an earthenware bowl, perfect for the monsoon season. This ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly recipe combines the flavors of Korea with the principles of Indian cuisine.

ingredients.name: monsoon greens
ingredients.notes: such as collard greens or mustard greens

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the double-rise-bread into 1-inch cubes and toast until crispy.
In a large bowl, whisk together the wholegrain mustard, korean chili flakes, and sesame oil.
Add the toasted bread cubes to the bowl and toss until the bread is evenly coated with the mustard mixture.
In a separate bowl, massage the monsoon greens with salt and black pepper until they are tender.
Add the fermented kimchi to the bowl with the greens and toss until well combined.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the bread mixture in the earthenware bowl, followed by a portion of the kimchi and greens mixture.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds on top of the dish and serve immediately.
Repeat the assembly process for each serving, garnishing with additional sesame seeds and chili flakes if desired.
